calendar.matters:
Renewed Motion To For Reconsideration-Due To A Clear Error And To Avoid Manifest Inujustice
Off Calendar Dec-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ted A. Galfin, Esq., 949.751.0148, for defendant Pick Your Parts; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF KENNETH KINNARD'S Renewed Motion To For Reconsideration Due To A Clear Error And To Avoid Manifest Injustice is DENIED. Plaintiff does not demonstrate good cause for relief under CCP 1008 or any other statute. Plaintiff has had a fair and full opportunity to present his case. It is too late and prejudicial to defendant to vacate the settlement that occurred on April 7, 2010. Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Off Calendar May-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ted A. Galfin, Esq., from Galfin, Passon & Greely, LLP, for Defendant and opposing party Pick Your Part

No appearance by plaintiff. Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF KENNETH KINNARD Motion To Vacate Voluntary Dismissal With Prejudice And To Void The Judgement/Dismissal is Denied. Plaintiff's statements to Judge Robertson on April 7, 2010 estop him from now asserting that he lacked capacity to enter into the settlement on that date. Prevailing party to send a form of order by e-mail. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
